# pydiylc

Python emitter / reader for DIYLC (DIY Layout Creator) `.diy` circuit-layout files. Write layouts in Python or JSON, read existing `.diy` files back, render to SVG/PNG, and serve everything to LLM clients over MCP. This document is the AI-friendly entry point тАФ everything an assistant needs to use the library without browsing the repo.

## What this library is

- Source of truth: the Java app at https://github.com/bancika/diy-layout-creator.
- pydiylc writes the same XML format DIYLC writes. Target: DIYLC 5.x (file format compatible back to v3, and v1 `<Layout>`-rooted files are parsed too).
- **100% component recognition** on the upstream DIYLC regression corpus (106,599/106,599 components across 709 layouts) with 709/709 perfect read тЖТ save тЖТ re-read round-trips.
- Coordinates are floats in **inches** by default. The DIYLC editor grid is **0.1 in**. Round positions to multiples of 0.1 unless you have a reason not to.
- Sizes (length, width, pin spacing, board spacing, hole size, etc.) are `Measure(value, unit)`. Helper constructors: `inches(v)`, `mm(v)`, `cm(v)`.

## Two ways to use it

1. **Direct Python / JSON** тАФ `Project` + dataclass components. Best when you control the runtime.
2. **MCP server** (`pydiylc-mcp`) тАФ 40 tools for catalog discovery, project edits, validation, undo/redo, and rendering. Best for chat-driven workflows.

The MCP path is described in detail below. Both paths are equivalent feature-wise.

## Minimal Python example

```python
from pydiylc import Project, PerfBoard, Resistor, SolderPad, CopperTrace

p = Project(title="Booster", width_cm=10, height_cm=8)
p.add(PerfBoard("Board1", x1=1.0, y1=1.0, x2=3.0, y2=2.5))
p.add(SolderPad("P1", x=1.2, y=1.2))
p.add(SolderPad("P2", x=1.2, y=1.8))
p.add(Resistor("R1", x1=1.2, y1=1.2, x2=1.2, y2=1.8, value="10K"))
p.add(CopperTrace("T1", points=[(1.2, 1.8), (2.0, 1.8)]))
p.save("booster.diy")
```

## Project

```python
Project(
    title: str = "New Project",
    author: str = "",
    width_cm: float = 29.0,
    height_cm: float = 21.0,
    grid_inches: float = 0.1,
    dot_spacing: int = 1,
    file_version: tuple[int, int, int] = (5, 7, 0),
)
```

`p.add(component)` appends; `p.save(path)` writes XML; `Project.read(path)` parses an existing `.diy` back.

## Components

All component constructors are dataclasses тАФ every field has a sensible default except positional anchors (`name`, coordinates).

### Discovering what's available

Don't memorize the list. Use `catalog.json` (at repo root, regenerated by `python -m pydiylc.catalog`) or, over MCP, `list_categories()` followed by `list_components_in_category(category)`. Categories follow the DIYLC class path:

- **boards** (9) тАФ `BlankBoard`, `Breadboard`, `EyeletBoard`, `MarshallPerfBoard`, `PerfBoard`, `TagStrip`, `TerminalStrip`, `TriPadBoard`, `VeroBoard`
- **passive** (20) тАФ `AudioTransformer`, `AxialElectrolyticCapacitor`, `AxialFilmCapacitor`, `CapacitorSymbol`, `CrystalOscillator`, `ElectrolyticCanCapacitor`, `FuseSymbol`, `InductorSymbol`, `MultiSectionCapacitor`, `PotentiometerPanel`, `PotentiometerSymbol`, `RadialCeramicDiskCapacitor`, `RadialElectrolytic`, `RadialFilmCapacitor`, `Resistor`, `ResistorSymbol`, `TantalumCapacitor`, `TransformerCoil`, `TransformerCore`, `TrimmerPotentiometer`
- **semiconductors** (18) тАФ `BJTSymbol`, `BridgeRectifier`, `DIL_IC`, `DiodeGlass`, `DiodePlastic`, `DiodeSymbol`, `ICSymbol`, `JFETSymbol`, `LED`, `LEDSymbol`, `PhotoDiodeSymbol`, `SIL_IC`, `SchottkyDiodeSymbol`, `TransistorTO1`, `TransistorTO126`, `TransistorTO220`, `TransistorTO92`, `ZenerDiodeSymbol`
- **connectivity** (11) тАФ `CopperTrace`, `CurvedTrace`, `Dot`, `Eyelet`, `GroundFill`, `HookupWire`, `Jumper`, `Line`, `SolderPad`, `TraceCut`, `Turret`
- **electromechanical** (14) тАФ `BatterySnap9V`, `CliffJack1_4`, `ClosedJack1_4`, `FuseHolderPanel`, `IECSocket`, `MiniRelay`, `MiniToggleSwitch`, `NeutrikJack1_4`, `OpenJack1_4`, `PilotLampHolder`, `PinHeader`, `PlasticDCJack`, `RCAJack`, `RotarySelectorSwitch`
- **tube** (4) тАФ `PentodeSymbol`, `TriodeSymbol`, `TubeDiodeSymbol`, `TubeSocket`
- **chassis** (3) тАФ `ChassisPanel`, `EllipticalCutout`, `RectangularCutout`
- **guitar** (7) тАФ `HumbuckerPickup`, `JazzBassPickup`, `LPSwitch`, `LeverSwitch`, `P90Pickup`, `PBassPickup`, `SingleCoilPickup`
- **shapes** (4) тАФ `Ellipse`, `Polygon`, `Rectangle`, `TapeMeasure`
- **smd** (2) тАФ `SMDCapacitor`, `SMDResistor`
- **misc** (7) тАФ `BOM`, `BatterySymbol`, `GroundSymbol`, `Image`, `Label`, `PCBText`, `WrapLabel`

For exact field lists, defaults, types, and allowed enum values, load **`catalog.json`** at repo root. It is the canonical machine-readable schema.

## JSON ingestion (preferred path for LLMs)

You don't have to write Python. Emit a JSON document and call `Project.from_dict(...)` or `Project.from_json(...)`:

```python
from pydiylc import Project

p = Project.from_dict({
    "title": "Booster",
    "width_cm": 10, "height_cm": 8,
    "components": [
        {"type": "PerfBoard", "name": "Board1", "x1": 1.0, "y1": 1.0, "x2": 3.0, "y2": 2.5},
        {"type": "TransistorTO92", "name": "Q1", "x": 1.5, "y": 1.3,
         "value": "2N5088", "pinout": "BJT_EBC"},
        {"type": "Resistor", "name": "R1", "x1": 1.5, "y1": 1.3,
         "x2": 1.5, "y2": 1.0, "value": "1M"},
        {"type": "DIL_IC", "name": "U1", "x": 2.0, "y": 1.5,
         "value": "TL072", "pin_count": "_8"},
        {"type": "PotentiometerPanel", "name": "VR1", "x": 1.0, "y": 4.0,
         "resistance": "100K", "taper": "LOG"}
    ]
})
p.save("booster.diy")
```

Each component dict needs a `type` field naming a pydiylc class. The rest are kwargs. Measure fields (`length`, `width`, `pin_spacing`, etc.) accept either `{"value": 0.1, "unit": "in"}` or a bare number (interpreted as inches).

### Discriminator collision: use `_type` for jacks and pads

Some components have a `type` *field of their own* (jack mono/stereo selector, solder pad shape, etc.). When you set that field, the dict has two `type` keys and Python/JSON keep only the last one тАФ meaning the class name gets silently dropped. **For these components, use `_type` for the class name and leave `type` for the field:**

```python
# WRONG тАФ the {'type': 'OpenJack1_4', ...} is overwritten by 'type': 'MONO'
{"type": "OpenJack1_4", "name": "J_in", "type": "MONO"}

# RIGHT тАФ '_type' carries the class name, 'type' is the field value
{"_type": "OpenJack1_4", "name": "J_in", "type": "MONO"}
```

Components affected (exhaustive list тАФ i.e. every component whose dataclass has a `type` field):

- **Jacks** тАФ `OpenJack1_4`, `ClosedJack1_4`, `CliffJack1_4`, `NeutrikJack1_4` (MONO/STEREO/SWITCHED)
- **Pads + boards** тАФ `SolderPad` (ROUND/SQUARE/OVAL_*), `BlankBoard` (SQUARE/ROUND)
- **Pickups** тАФ `SingleCoilPickup` (Stratocaster/Telecaster/etc.), `HumbuckerPickup` (PAF/DOG_EAR/MINI), `P90Pickup` (DOG_EAR/SOAP_BAR)
- **Switches** тАФ `LeverSwitch` (DP3T/DP3T_5pos/DPDT/_4P5T/_3P3T)
- **Pots** тАФ `PotentiometerPanel`, `TrimmerPotentiometer` (ThroughHole/PCB)
- **Symbols** тАФ `GroundSymbol` (DEFAULT/TRIANGLE)

**Rule of thumb: when in doubt, use `_type` everywhere тАФ it always works** and side-steps every possible collision.

### Self-correcting errors

The loader and MCP tools surface errors that an LLM can act on directly:

- **Typo in a class name** (`'RadialCeramicCapacitor'` for `RadialCeramicDiskCapacitor`) тЖТ error includes `Did you mean: RadialCeramicDiskCapacitor?`
- **Typo in a field name** (`value` on a `ElectrolyticCanCapacitor` whose field is `values`) тЖТ error includes `Did you mean 'values', 'voltage'?` plus the full accepted-field list
- **Wrong coordinate shape** (`x`/`y` on a two-point class, or `x1`/`y1`/`x2`/`y2` on a single-anchor class) тЖТ error says which shape the class wants
- **Component name miss in lookups** (`get_component('R3')` when the real name is `R03`) тЖТ error suggests close matches from the project
- **Project ID miss** тЖТ same, suggests close matches across loaded projects

All of these arrive in the `error` string returned by `add_components` per-item entries or raised by single-item tools, so a retry loop can self-correct without human intervention.

## Value strings

Two-pin components parse their `value` string as `number + unit`:

- Resistor: `"4.7K"`, `"1M"`, `"470R"`. Bare number тЖТ `K`.
- Film cap: `"100nF"`, `"1uF"`. Bare тЖТ `nF`.
- Ceramic cap: `"100pF"`, `"1000pF"`. Bare тЖТ `pF`.
- Electrolytic: `"22uF"`, `"470uF"`. Bare тЖТ `uF`.

The parser is **tolerant** тАФ European notation (`"3,3K"`, `"2k2"`, `"4M7"`) and combined specs (`"25 V 2500uF"`) all work. Pure free text (`"ceramic"`, `"matched"`) parses as zero with the default unit and keeps the original string for display.

## Enums тАФ the values you must respect

The catalog's `enum_pool` is the source of truth (use `list_enums()` over MCP to discover them). The high-frequency ones for pedal/amp work:

- `power` (Resistor): `QUARTER`, `HALF`, `ONE`, `TWO`
- `voltage` (Capacitors): `_4V`, `_6V3`, `_10V`, `_16V`, `_25V`, `_35V`, `_50V`, `_63V`, `_100V`, `_160V`, `_200V`, `_250V`, `_400V`, `_450V`, `_600V`, `_1kV`
- `display`: `NAME`, `VALUE`, `NONE`, `BOTH` (transistors also accept `PINOUT`)
- `orientation`: `DEFAULT`, `_90`, `_180`, `_270`
- `pinout` (TO92/TO1/TO126/TO220): `BJT_EBC`, `BJT_CBE`, `BJT_BCE`, `BJT_ECB`, `JFET_*`, `MOSFET_*`, `REGULATOR_*` тАФ see catalog
- `pin_count` (DIL_IC): `_4`, `_6`, `_8`, `_10`, ... `_50` (even values only)
- `pin_count` (SIL_IC): `_3`, `_4`, ... `_51`
- `taper` (PotentiometerPanel): `LIN`, `LOG`, `REV_LOG`, `W`, `S`, `M`, `N`
- `switch_type` (MiniToggleSwitch): `SPST`, `SPDT`, `SPDT_off`, `DPDT`, `DPDT_off`, `DPDT_ononon_1/2`, `_3PDT`, `_3PDT_off`, `_4PDT`, `_4PDT_off`, `_4PDT_ononon_1/2`, `_5PDT`, `_5PDT_off`. Use `_3PDT` for a true-bypass foot switch.
- `polarity` (PlasticDCJack): `NONE`, `CENTER_POSITIVE`, `CENTER_NEGATIVE` (most pedals are CENTER_NEGATIVE)
- `type` (OpenJack1_4 / ClosedJack1_4 / NeutrikJack1_4): `MONO`, `STEREO`, `SWITCHED`
- `gauge` (HookupWire AWG): `_12` ... `_30`
- `type` (SolderPad): `ROUND`, `SQUARE`, `OVAL_HORIZONTAL`, `OVAL_VERTICAL`
- `style` (Jumper, HookupWire, etc.): `SOLID`, `DASHED`, `DOTTED`
- `type` (HumbuckerPickup): `PAF`, `DOG_EAR`, `MINI`
- `type` (LeverSwitch): `DP3T`, `DP3T_5pos`, `DPDT`, `_4P5T`, `_3P3T`
- `size` (SMD): `_0201`, `_0402`, `_0603`, `_0805`, `_1206`, `_1210`, `_1812`, `_2010`, `_2512`

All enum violations raise `ValueError` with the full allowed list in the message.

## Coordinate conventions

- Origin top-left, y increases downward (matches DIYLC).
- Two-pin components take `x1,y1,x2,y2`. Library inserts the midpoint automatically.
- `CopperTrace` / `GroundFill` / `Polygon` etc. take a list of `(x, y)` tuples.
- `HookupWire` takes 2 endpoints (or any explicit polyline of тЙе2 points).
- Snap to the 0.1-inch grid unless you specifically need fine positioning. `snap_to_grid()` over MCP cleans up off-grid coords automatically.

## Colors

Hex string (no `#`) or an int. Examples: `"ffe303"`, `"abc"`, `0xff0000`.

## SVG / PNG preview

```python
from pydiylc.svg import render_svg, render_svg_file
render_svg_file(project, "preview.svg")
svg_text = render_svg(project)

from pydiylc.cairo_render import render_png  # needs pycairo
render_png(project, "preview.png", dpi=144)
```

The renderer is a quick-feedback preview, not pixel-identical to DIYLC. For high-fidelity output, run DIYLC headless on the saved `.diy`:

```
diylc -convert layout.diy layout.png
```

## MCP tool surface

`pydiylc-mcp` exposes **40 tools** plus 2 resources and 2 prompts. The project state lives in memory keyed by `project_id` (defaulting to `"default"`); multiple parallel projects supported.

### Catalog browsing (no project state needed)

- `list_categories()` тЖТ `[{category, count}]` тАФ cheap discovery.
- `list_components_in_category(category)` тЖТ component summaries with one-line docs.
- `list_component_types()` тЖТ full catalog (large; prefer the above for discovery).
- `describe_component_type(type_name)` тЖТ one component's complete schema.
- `list_enums()` тЖТ every enum the catalog references with counts.
- `enum_values(enum_name)` тЖТ allowed values for one enum.

### Project lifecycle

- `create_project(project_id, title, width_cm, height_cm)`
- `create_project_from_dict(payload, project_id)` тАФ one-shot build from JSON.
- `list_projects()`, `delete_project(project_id)`
- `set_project_metadata(project_id, title=, author=, width_cm=, height_cm=)`
- `read_diy(path, project_id)` тАФ parse an existing `.diy` into a slot.

### Inspection

- `list_components(project_id)` тАФ anchors + value summary.
- `get_component(name, project_id)` тАФ full field dict.
- `find_components(query, project_id, limit=20)` тАФ fzf-style fuzzy match.
- `get_pins(name, project_id)` тЖТ `[{pin, label, x, y}]` for every control point.
- `stats(project_id)` тЖТ `{components, by_type, bbox_in, canvas_in, ...}`.

### Edits (every mutating tool records an undo snapshot)

- `add_component(component, project_id, dry_run=False)` тАФ `component` is `{type, kwargsтАж}`.
- `add_components(components, project_id, stop_on_error=False)` тАФ **batch add**, returns per-item errors without failing the batch.
- `remove_component(name, project_id)`.
- `move_component(name, dx, dy, project_id)` тАФ connection-aware: drags mounted components / stretches wires.
- `move_node(name, point_index, dx, dy)` тАФ single-point detach move.
- `move_node_to(name, point_index, x, y)` тАФ snap-to-target.
- `rotate_component(name, clockwise, project_id)` тАФ cycles `orientation` enum if present, otherwise rotates coords.
- `duplicate_component(name, new_name?, dx, dy, project_id)` тАФ auto-increments names.
- `set_value(name, value, field?, project_id)` тАФ auto-picks the primary value field (value/text/resistance/tube_type).
- `set_field(name, field, value, project_id, dry_run=False)` тАФ set any dataclass attribute. Type coercion for primitives; `{value, unit}` for Measures.
- `set_fields(name, fields: dict, project_id, dry_run=False)` тАФ **atomic multi-field update**; either all changes commit or none do.
- `add_wire(src, dst, name?, color, project_id)` тАФ bare HookupWire from `[x, y]` to `[x, y]`.
- `connect(from_name, to_name, from_pin?, to_pin?, kind, color, name?, project_id)` тАФ wire two named components together. Omit pin indices and the nearest pair is chosen automatically. `kind` is `"wire"` (HookupWire) or `"trace"` (CopperTrace).
- `align(names, axis, mode, project_id, dry_run=False)` тАФ line N components up. `axis` is `"x"` / `"y"`, `mode` is `"first"` / `"mean"` / `"min"` / `"max"`.
- `snap_to_grid(name?, grid=0.1, project_id, dry_run=False)` тАФ snap one or every component to a grid (default 0.1 in).

### Validation

- `validate(project_id)` тЖТ `{ok, components, issues: [{kind, name, message, тАж}]}` тАФ catches duplicate names, missing names, off-canvas geometry. Run before `save`.

### History (mirrors the GUI viewer's undo stack)

- `undo(project_id)` тЖТ `{undone, components, can_undo, can_redo}`.
- `redo(project_id)` тЖТ same shape as undo.
- `history(project_id)` тЖТ `{depth, redo_depth, last_label, can_undo, can_redo}`.

### I/O

- `save(path?, project_id, return_content=False)` тАФ write `.diy` to disk, or omit `path` (or set `return_content=True`) to get the XML inline under both `content` and `xml` keys.
- `render_svg(path?, project_id, dpi=96, return_content=False)` тАФ same pattern; inline returns SVG text under both `content` and `svg` keys.
- `render_png(path?, project_id, dpi=96, return_content=False)` тАФ inline returns base64-encoded bytes under `content_base64`.
- `to_json(project_id)` тЖТ round-trip-clean JSON dict.

### Resources + prompts

- Resource `pydiylc://catalog` тАФ the catalog JSON.
- Resource `pydiylc://llms.txt` тАФ this guide.
- Prompt `build_pedal_layout(name)` тАФ guitar effects pedal walkthrough.
- Prompt `build_guitar_wiring(name)` тАФ Strat-style 3-pickup wiring walkthrough.
- Prompt `build_amp_psu(name)` тАФ tube-amp power-supply walkthrough.
- Prompt `modify_existing_layout(diy_path)` тАФ read + tweak workflow.

## LLM workflow patterns

These are battle-tested over the MCP tool surface. Use the closest one as a template.

### Pattern: "Build a layout from scratch"

```
1. list_categories()                          # see what exists
2. list_components_in_category('passive')     # narrow to what you need
3. create_project('demo', width_cm=10, height_cm=8)
4. add_components([...])                      # one batch call for 20-50 parts
5. connect('R1', 'Q1')                        # wire by name (nearest pins)
6. align(['P1','P2','P3'], axis='y')          # tidy up
7. snap_to_grid()                             # clean off-grid coords
8. validate()                                 # check for issues
9. render_svg(return_content=True)            # inline preview
10. save('out.diy')
```

### Pattern: "Read and tweak"

```
1. read_diy('input.diy', project_id='layout')
2. stats('layout')                            # quick summary
3. find_components('R3')                      # fuzzy lookup
4. set_fields('R3', {'value': '47K', 'alpha': 80})
5. move_component('R3', dx=0.5, dy=0)
6. validate()
7. save('output.diy')
```

### Pattern: "Undo a misstep"

```
1. add_component({...})                       # turns out to be wrong
2. undo('default')                            # back to previous state
3. add_component({...different...})
```

### Pattern: "Sandboxed chat (no disk access)"

```
1. create_project(...)
2. add_components([...])
3. render_svg(return_content=True)            # SVG markup inline
4. save(return_content=True)                  # .diy XML inline
```

### Tips

- **Errors are corrective**: typos in class names and field names both surface `Did you mean X?` hints. Coordinate-shape errors say which shape the class wants. Use them тАФ a retry loop with the suggested fix usually succeeds.
- **Dry-run first** when an action is destructive or affects many components тАФ `add_component`, `set_field`, `set_fields`, `snap_to_grid`, `align` all support `dry_run=True`.
- **Per-item errors don't kill batches** тАФ `add_components` returns errors alongside the components that succeeded. Loop with `stop_on_error=True` only if you need atomic behavior.
- **Multi-pin switch/jack layouts**: MiniToggleSwitch lays out N-pole ├Ч 3-throw lugs as a real 3├ЧN grid (3PDT = 3├Ч3, DPDT = 2├Ч3), not a single column тАФ so wires coming from different physical directions can land on different lugs via the `connect` nearest-pin heuristic. Use `get_pins(name)` to see the actual pin coordinates.

## Command-line interface

```bash
pydiylc convert layout.py layout.diy
pydiylc convert layout.diy layout.json
pydiylc convert layout.json layout.svg
pydiylc render  layout.diy --out preview.svg --dpi 144
pydiylc info    layout.diy   # title, count, by-type, warnings
```

Sources: `.py` (`project=` or `build()` or `main()` returning a Project), `.json` (the same schema documented above), or `.diy`. Targets: `.diy`, `.json`, `.svg`.

## GTK4 viewer (`pydiylc-view`)

```bash
pydiylc-view path/to/layout.py
```

Wayland-native window, mouse pan/scroll-zoom, click to select, T to enter the keyboard tree editor (vim-style nav, full undo/redo, /-fuzzy-jump, value-edit, duplicate, rotate, connection-aware moves with diff-on-save). Requires `pip install pydiylc[viewer]` plus system GTK 4. Dark theme is available via the gear icon тЖТ Preferences тЖТ Theme (Light / Dark / System).

## Reading .diy files

```python
from pydiylc import Project, read_warnings

p = Project.read("some_pedal.diy")
print(p.title, len(p.components))
for w in read_warnings(p):
    print("тЪа", w)  # very rare now тАФ corpus recognition is 100%
```

Reader handles **three** on-disk formats:

- Modern `<project>` root (DIYLC 4.x+).
- v3 XStream `<org.diylc.core.Project>` root with nested measures (`<size><value>тАж</value><unit>тАж</unit></size>`) and cross-element references (`<x reference="../../path"/>`).
- Legacy v1 `<Layout>` root (DIYLC 1.x/2.x) with integer perfboard-hole coordinates and attribute-only elements. An implicit board (PerfBoard / VeroBoard / BlankBoard) is synthesized from `Layout.Type`.

## Where to look next

- `catalog.json` тАФ full schema for codegen / validation.
- `examples/demo_lpb1_stripboard.py` тАФ complete LPB-1 booster layout.
- `examples/demo_render_svg.py` тАФ programmatic render.
- `src/pydiylc/components.py` тАФ every component's docstring + the XML it emits.
- `src/pydiylc/enums.py` тАФ single source of truth for enum values.
